perturbation.original_text: This Termination Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the PRC, without regard to conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Termination Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from PRC to Delaware could create a legal contradiction if the original agreement had clauses specifically tailored to PRC law, such as those relating to internet content or data privacy. Delaware law might not adequately address issues unique to the PRC.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 126 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 126 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China allows parties to choose the law applicable to their contract, but only in contracts involving foreign elements. The original agreement was likely drafted with PRC law in mind given the parties and subject matter. Switching to Delaware law introduces potential inconsistencies with regulatory requirements specific to the PRC that the original agreement adhered to.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Detailed Rules for the Sanitation Registration of Factories/ Storehouses of Food for Export [Expired] 出口食品厂、库卫生注册细则 [失效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Other Institutions of the Central Government , Others , All Other Administrations , State Administration of Entry-Exit Inspection & Quarantine (former State Commodity Inspection & Quarantine） Date issued： 11-14-1994 Effective date: 11-14-1994 Level of Authority: Departmental Regulatory Documents Area of Law: Commodity Inspection, Animal and Plant Quarantine Invalidated by： Administrative Provisions on the Sanitation Registration and List Entry of Export Food Manufacturing Enterprises (Issued on 04-19-2002 Effective on 05-20-2002) DETAILED RULES FOR THE SANITATION REGISTRATION OF FACTORIES/STOREHOUSES OF FOOD FOR EXPORT (Promulgated by the State Administration of Import & Export Commodity Inspection of the People's Republic of China on November 14, 1994) 出口食品厂、库卫生注册细则 （１９９４年１１月１４日国家进出口商品检验局发布） CHAPTER 1 GENERAL PRINCIPLES 第一章 总则 Article 1 These detailed rules are formulated in accordance with the regulations concerned in the Law of the People's Republic of China on Import & Export Commodity Inspection , the law of the People's Republic of China on food Sanitation (for trial implementation), Provisions of the People's Republic of China on Sanitation of Food for Export (for trial implementation) and Regulations of the Implementation of the Law of the People's Republic of China on Import and Export Commodity Inspection . 第一条 根据《 中华人民共和国进出口商品检验法 》、《 中华人民共和国食品卫生法（试行） 》、《中华人民共和国出口食品卫生管理办法》以及《 中华人民共和国进出口商品检验法实施条例 》的有关规定，制定本细则。 Article 2 The State implements the system of sanitation registration and list entry of factories/storehouses which produce, process or store food for export. The factories/storehouses engaged in manufacturing, processing and storage of food for export (hereinafter referred to as factories/storehouses of food for export) in the People's Republic of China can be allowed to manufacture, process and store food for export only after certificate of sanitation registration or list entry is issued to them. 第二条 国家对出口食品生产、加工、储存厂、库实施卫生注册、登记制度。 凡在中华人民共和国境内生产、加工、储存出口食品厂、库（以下统称出口食品厂、库）必须取得卫生注册或卫生登记证书方可生产、加工、储存出口食品。 Article 3 The State Administration of Import and Export Commodity Inspection of the People's Republic of China (hereinafter referred to as the State Administration of Commodity Inspection in the provinces, autonomous regions and municipalities directly under the central government (hereinafter referred to as commodity inspection authorities) shall be responsible for the said registration or list entry work under their jurisdiction. 第三条 国家商检局主管全国出口食品厂、库卫生注册、登记工作。国家商检局设在各省、自治区、直辖市的商检局（以下简称商检机构）负责所辖地区出口食品厂、库的卫生注册、登记工作。 Article 4 The following factories/storehouses should be subjected to the administration of sanitation registration: The factories/storehouses engaged in the manufacturing or storage of export canned food, beverages, poultry and animals meat as well as their products, aquatic products (excluding live products) and its by- products, quick frozen food, dairy products, eggs, honey and their products, dehydrated food, flour products with sugar, sugar, teas and casings; while other factories/storehouses of food for export should be entered into list to be subject to sanitary control. 第四条 凡生产罐头类，饮料类，畜禽肉及其制品类，水产品（活品除外）及其制品类，速冻食品类，乳、蛋、蜂蜜制品类，脱水食品类，面糖制品类，糖类，茶叶类，肠衣类的出口食品厂、库实施卫生注册管理；其他出口食品厂、库实施卫生登记管理。 CHAPTER 2 THE APPLICATION FOR SANITATION REGISTRATION 第二章 卫生注册的申请 Article 5 The factories/storehouses of food for export which apply for sanitation registration should establish quality system on sanitation on the basis of Sanitary Requiremen
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from PRC to Delaware, potentially creating a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation states that Article 126 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China allows parties to choose the law applicable to their contract only in contracts involving foreign elements and that the agreement was likely drafted with PRC law in mind. Switching to Delaware law could introduce inconsistencies with regulatory requirements specific to the PRC. Scraped Law Snippet 2, concerning sanitation registration for export food factories in the PRC, exemplifies the specific regulatory environment the original contract may have been tailored to. Therefore, the shift in governing law could contradict the original intent and regulatory context of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Beijing SINA and SINA Leju agree that, upon the date of execution of this Termination Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect, and, for the avoidance of doubt, no provisions of the Original Agreement survive such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Beijing SINA and SINA Leju agree that, upon the date of execution of this Termination Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect; however, provisions related to intellectual property rights from the original agreement shall continue in full force and effect indefinitely, even after the termination of this agreement, as determined by Beijing SINA.
perturbation.explanation: While parties can agree to terminate an agreement, specifying that IP rights remain in effect "as determined by Beijing SINA" introduces an element of ambiguity and unilateral control that could conflict with legal standards regarding fair dealing and contract interpretation. IP rights, normally, would need to be stated in writing to be transferred or retained.
perturbation.contradicted_law: PRC Contract Law - Article 6 on Fairness
perturbation.law_citation: Article 6 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China mandates that parties follow the principle of fairness in exercising their rights and fulfilling their obligations. Giving one party unilateral control over the indefinite continuation of intellectual property rights, departing from the termination agreement's general intent, contradicts this principle and increases the risk of unenforceable contract terms.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: e of sanitation registration or list entry is issued to them. 第二条 国家对出口食品生产、加工、储存厂、库实施卫生注册、登记制度。 凡在中华人民共和国境内生产、加工、储存出口食品厂、库（以下统称出口食品厂、库）必须取得卫生注册或卫生登记证书方可生产、加工、储存出口食品。 Article 3 The State Administration of Import and Export Commodity Inspection of the People's Republic of China (hereinafter referred to as the State Administration of Commodity Inspection in the provinces, autonomous regions and municipalities directly under the central government (hereinafter referred to as commodity inspection authorities) shall be responsible for the said registration or list entry work under their jurisdiction. 第三条 国家商检局主管全国出口食品厂、库卫生注册、登记工作。国家商检局设在各省、自治区、直辖市的商检局（以下简称商检机构）负责所辖地区出口食品厂、库的卫生注册、登记工作。 Article 4 The following factories/storehouses should be subjected to the administration of sanitation registration: The factories/storehouses engaged in the manufacturing or storage of export canned food, beverages, poultry and animals meat as well as their products, aquatic products (excluding live products) and its by- products, quick frozen food, dairy products, eggs, honey and their products, dehydrated food, flour products with sugar, sugar, teas and casings; while other factories/storehouses of food for export should be entered into list to be subject to sanitary control. 第四条 凡生产罐头类，饮料类，畜禽肉及其制品类，水产品（活品除外）及其制品类，速冻食品类，乳、蛋、蜂蜜制品类，脱水食品类，面糖制品类，糖类，茶叶类，肠衣类的出口食品厂、库实施卫生注册管理；其他出口食品厂、库实施卫生登记管理。 CHAPTER 2 THE APPLICATION FOR SANITATION REGISTRATION 第二章 卫生注册的申请 Article 5 The factories/storehouses of food for export which apply for sanitation registration should establish quality system on sanitation on the basis of Sanitary Requirements for Factories/Storehouses of food for Export (hereinafter referred to as Sanitary Requirements ) and should meet the requirements concerned in the Standard on Sanitation of Factories/Storehouses of Food for Export (hereinafter referred to as Sanitary Standard). 第五条 申请卫生注册的出口食品厂、库必须按照《 出口食品厂、库卫生要求 》（以下简称 《卫生要求》 ）建立卫生质量体系，并应当达到有关出口食品厂、库的卫生规范（以下简称《卫生规范》）规定的要求。 Article 6 The factories/storehouses of food for report should fill the application form (in three copies) for sanitation registration and submit them to the commodity inspection authority before they begin to manufacture food for export. ．．．．．． 第六条 出口食品厂、库应当在生产出口食品前，向商检机构申请卫生注册，并填写卫生注册申请书（一式三份）。 Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the principle of fairness mandated by Article 6 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China (as stated in the Law Explanation). The original text ensures the termination of the agreement and all its provisions, whereas the changed text allows Beijing SINA to unilaterally determine the continuation of intellectual property rights, creating an imbalance. This directly opposes the fairness principle and increases the risk of an unenforceable contract, constituting an inconsistency and creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Termination Agreement represents the complete, integrated, and entire agreement between the parties, and may not be modified except in writing signed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Termination Agreement represents the complete, integrated, and entire agreement between the parties and may not be modified except through oral agreement, provided that Beijing SINA consents to such modification.
perturbation.explanation: Most jurisdictions require contract modifications to be in writing, especially when the original agreement specifies that modifications must be in writing. Allowing oral modifications, especially with one party's consent being the sole condition, creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Statute of Frauds - Requirements for Written Agreements
perturbation.law_explanation: While Article 11 does allow for contracts to be formed verbally if written form is not required by law, many types of commercial contracts, particularly termination agreements that alter pre-existing written contracts, must be in writing to be enforceable. Changing the clause to allow oral modification contradicts this principle, especially when the original contract required written modification. The provision would become vulnerable to unenforceability if challenged.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates written modifications, while the changed text permits oral modifications, provided Beijing SINA consents. This directly contradicts the principle that modifications to written agreements, especially termination agreements, should also be in writing to be enforceable, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. Although the snippets provided do not explicitly mention written modification requirements, the Law Explanation asserts this is a common principle in commercial contracts, especially for termination agreements that alter pre-existing written contracts. The modification undermines the enforceability of the agreement, creating a direct contradiction by opening the door to potential disputes over oral agreements and their validity. This undermines the original intent of having all modifications in writing, creating a clear contradiction.
